descriptions.Accounting: Skill of balancing books, creating false books, and identifying them, juggling numbers, creating budgets and handling day-to-day business operations.
descriptions.Acting: Skill of assuming a role, disguising yourself as someone else, whether real or fictitious, and faking emotions and moods.
descriptions.Air Vehicle Tech: Skill of repairing and maintaining air vehicles. The DV and amount of time it takes you to repair a damaged/ destroyed vehicle with this Skill to perfect condition is based on the amount of damage it has sustained. (see sidebar on Page 140).
descriptions.Animal Handling: Skill of handling, training, and caring for animals.
descriptions.Anthropology: Skill of understanding human cultures, societies, and behavior across groups and eras.
descriptions.Archery: Skill for accurately firing bolt- or arrow-launching projectile weapons.
descriptions.Athletics: Skill of jumping, climbing, throwing, swimming, lifting weights, etc. It combines the basic elements of any high school-level sports program.
descriptions.Autofire: Skill for controlling automatic fire and burst weapons accurately.
descriptions.Basic Tech: Skill of identifying, understanding, and repairing simple electronic devices and all other items not covered by another TECH Skill. The DV and amount of time it takes you to repair a damaged/destroyed item with this Skill to perfect condition is based on the Price Category of the item (see sidebar on Page 140).
descriptions.Brawling: Skill at fighting and grappling with brute strength.
descriptions.Bribery: Skill of knowing when to bribe someone, how to approach them, and how much to offer.
descriptions.Bureaucracy: Skill for dealing with bureaucrats, cutting red tape, knowing who to talk to in a bureaucracy, how to reach them, and how to extract information from bureaucracies.
descriptions.Business: Skill regarding knowledge of basic business practices, laws of supply and demand, employee management, procurement, sales, and marketing.
descriptions.Composition: The required Skill for writing songs, articles, or stories.
descriptions.Conceal/Reveal Object: Skill for hiding objects and finding objects that have been hidden. This is the Skill used for concealing weapons under clothing and detecting concealed weapons.
descriptions.Concentration: Skill of focus and mental control, encompassing feats of memory, recall, and physiological control.
descriptions.Contortionist: Skill of manipulating your body to get out of handcuffs or other bindings and to fit yourself into otherwise inaccessible places or spaces.
descriptions.Conversation: Skill of extracting information from people without alerting them through careful conversation.
descriptions.Criminology: Skill of discovering clues by dusting for fingerprints, doing ballistic tests, examining evidence, and searching through police records and files.
descriptions.Cryptography: Skill of encrypting and decoding messages.
descriptions.Cybertech: Skill of identifying, understanding, and repairing cybernetics. The DV and amount of time it takes you to repair a damaged/ destroyed item with this Skill to perfect condition is based on the Price Category of the item. (see sidebar on Page 140).
descriptions.Dance: Skill of dancing at a professional level.
descriptions.Deduction: Skill of taking several clues and leaping to a non-obvious conclusion or medical diagnosis.
descriptions.Demolitions: Skill of placing, handling, and disarming explosives and demolition charges.
descriptions.Drive Land Vehicle: Skill of driving and maneuvering land vehicles.
descriptions.Education: Skill of general knowledge, equivalent to a basic school education, allowing you to know how to read, write, use basic math, and know enough history to get by.
descriptions.Electronics/Security Tech: Skill of installing, bypassing, and repairing electronic and security systems.
descriptions.Endurance: Skill of withstanding harsh environmental conditions and hardship. This is also the ability to withstand pain or discomfort, particularly over long periods of time, by knowing the best ways to conserve strength and energy. Endurance Skill Checks are made whenever a Character must continue to be active after a long period without food, sleep, or water or after prologed activity.
descriptions.Evasion: Skill for getting out of the way of someone attacking you in melee. A Character with REF 8 or higher can also use this Skill to dodge Ranged Attacks and Explosions.
descriptions.First Aid: Skill of applying medical treatments to a wounded person to keep them from dying, and treating the most common Critical Injuries.
descriptions.Forgery: Skill of creating and detecting false documents, works of art, and identification. Forgery is most often opposed by Forgery.
descriptions.Gamble: Skill of knowing how to figure odds and play games of chance successfully.
descriptions.Handgun: Skill for accurately firing handheld projectile weapons.
descriptions.Heavy Weapons: Skill for operating and firing heavy support weapons safely and effectively.
descriptions.Human Perception: Skill of reading a person's facial expressions and body language to discern their emotional state and detect lies or deception.
descriptions.Instrument: Skill of performing music proficiently with a chosen instrument.
descriptions.Interrogation: Skill of forcibly extracting information from people.
descriptions.Land Vehicle Tech: Skill of repairing and maintaining vehicles that move on land. The DV and amount of time it takes you to repair a damaged/destroyed vehicle with this Skill to perfect condition is based on the amount of damage it has sustained. (see sidebar on Page 140).
descriptions.Language: Skill of speaking, reading, and writing a specific language fluently.
descriptions.Library Search: Skill of using databases, the Data Pool, libraries, and other compiled information sources to find facts.
descriptions.Lip Reading: Skill of reading someone's lips to tell what they're saying.
descriptions.Local Expert: Skill of knowing a specific area well and knowing the agendas of its various factions, both political and criminal. You must choose a specific location whenever you increase this Skill, which cannot be any larger than a single neighborhood or community.
descriptions.Martial Arts: Skill for advanced unarmed combat techniques and trained striking styles.
descriptions.Melee Weapon: Skill for effectively attacking and defending with hand-held melee weapons.
descriptions.Paint/Draw/Sculpt: Skill of producing professional paintings, drawings, or sculpture.
descriptions.Paramedic: Skill of advanced emergency medical care beyond basic first aid.
descriptions.Perception: Skill of spotting hidden things, like clues, traps, and people using the Stealth Skill, but not objects hidden with the Conceal/Reveal Object Skill.
descriptions.Personal Grooming: Skill of presenting yourself well through hygiene, styling, and social appearance.
descriptions.Persuasion: Skill of convincing, persuading, or influencing individuals. Also, the ability to talk others into doing what you want. This may be used individually or on large groups.
descriptions.Photography/Film: Skill for producing professional photographs, motion pictures, or braindances.
descriptions.Pick Lock: Skill for picking non-electronic locks and countering non-electronic security measures.
descriptions.Pick Pocket: Skill of discreetly stealing small items from a person without being noticed.
descriptions.Pilot Air Vehicle: Skill of piloting and maneuvering air vehicles.
descriptions.Pilot Sea Vehicle: Skill of piloting and maneuvering sea vehicles.
descriptions.Resist Torture/Drugs: Skill of resisting painful effects, including interrogation, torture, and drugs.
descriptions.Riding: Skill of riding a living creature trained for the purpose.
descriptions.Science: Skill for knowing how to design experiments, write scientific papers, test hypotheses, and discuss with other academics in a particular field of science. An area of study must be specified whenever this Skill is increased. Possible options include : Geology, Mathematics, Physics, Zoology, Anthropology, Biology, Chemistry, and History.
descriptions.Sea Vehicle Tech: Skill of repairing and maintaining sea vehicles. The DV and amount of time it takes you to repair a damaged/ destroyed vehicle with this Skill to perfect condition is based on the amount of damage it has sustained (see sidebar on Page 140).
descriptions.Shoulder Arms: Skill for firing shoulder-braced projectile weapons.
descriptions.Stealth: Skill of moving quietly, hiding, doing an action discreetly, or otherwise evading detection. Other Characters can try to find you with their Perception Skill.
descriptions.Streetwise: Skill of knowing where to get illegal and contraband things, talking to the criminal element, and avoiding bad situations in bad neighborhoods.
descriptions.Tactics: Skill of managing a large-scale battle effectively and efficiently. A Character with this Skill usually knows what must be done to direct a battle, and how an enemy force may react.
descriptions.Tracking: Skill of following a trail by observing tracks and other clues left behind.
descriptions.Trading: Skill of negotiating prices, evaluating value, and closing profitable deals.
descriptions.Wardrobe & Style: Skill of knowing the right clothes to wear, when to wear them, and how to look cool even in a spacesuit.
descriptions.Weaponstech: Skill for repairing and maintaining weapons of all types. The DV and amount of time it takes you to repair a damaged/destroyed item with this Skill to perfect condition is based on the Price Category of the item: (see sidebar on Page 140).
descriptions.Wilderness Survival: Skill for knowing how to survive comfortably in the wilderness. This knowledge includes how to set traps, forage for wood, track game, build shelters, and make fires.
